def: adj. Much, many, very, abundant.
adj. & s.m. = bisailā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Poisonous, venomous (syn. zahrīlā); — venomous reptile; snake; phlegmon or boil on the hand (bisahrī).
adj. Many, much, very, abundant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بسیار (bisyâr, “much, many, very”), from Middle Persian [script needed] (wsy(k)ʾl /⁠wasyār⁠/, “much, many”).

def: s.f. Abundance, superfluity.
s.f. Grazing fee charged by herdsmen, demand on each head of cattle paid to the cowherd, a kind of agistment
more: n. f. Grazing fee charged by herdsmen; the demand on each head of cattle paid to the cowherd; a kind of agistment. Guāl-bīt jo de nāhīṅ, maryo us ke ḍhor, Ghar ghātan ko lūṭ-kar, le jāīṅ din māṅ chor! Hīṛo. Song. Cursed be the man who grazing fee withholds and will not pay! May all his cattle die, be sacked his house in open day!
n. f. S. विट. 1. The dung or excrement of birds. 2. A factitious salt containing sulphur.
subs: s.m. Field or land grazed on by agistment; — land cultivated by forced labour.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیت (bayt), from Arabic بَيْت (bayt).

def: s.f. = bīṭh, q.v. s.f. Excrement, dung (especially of birds).
more: s. f. ( विटू ) 1. Dung of any animal. 2. (Same as विटलवण ) A factitious salt containing sulphur.

ety: S. विट, rt. विट्; or विड
def: s.f. A factitious salt containing sulphur ( = biṭ-lavaṇ).
s.f. = bīt, q.v. s.f. Grazing fee charged by herdsmen, demand on each head of cattle paid to the cowherd, a kind of agistment
subs: s.m. Field or land grazed on by agistment; — land cultivated by forced labour.

def: s.m. Span = bittā, q.v. s.m. Span, hand's breadth.
more: n. m. S. वटु a boy. 1. A son; boy; child; darling. 1. Jaisā sūt waisā pheṭā, jaisa bāp waisā beṭā . Prov. The cloth as the thread, the son as the sire. 2. Maiṅ to beṭā de-ke beṭī letā hūṅ . 2. A form of address used by Faqīrs towards their Chelās (disciples), etc. beṭā banānā , v. n. To adopt a son ( god lenā ). beṭā beṭī , n. m. Children; offspring; issue. Dunyā meṅ do hī chīz haiṅ, beṭā yā beṭī . Prov. Two blessings all this world affords, a daughter or a son accords. (Said to console the parents to whom a daughter has been born when they would rather have had a son). beṭe pote-wālā , n. m. beṭe pote-wālī , n. f. 1. A grandfather or grandmother. 2. A man or woman blest with children. beṭe-wālā , n. m. beṭe-wālī , n. f. The father or mother of the bridegroom. beṭā-khāū , n. m. A father who survives his son.

def: v.t. To spread, &c. = bithārnā, biṭānā, biṭlānā, q.q.v.; to put (food) into (the mouth), to taste (food); e.g. muṅh biṭālnā.
v. a. To taste (as food). Mūṅh biṭāl-ke jāiyo . Wom.

s.m. Awning, canopy, &c. = vi-tān, q.v. adj. Empty, vacant; pithless, sapless; dull, stupid; — wicked, abandoned; — s.m. Stretching, spreading out; expansion; — an awning, a canopy, cover; a cushion; — warp (of cloth); — an oblation, a sacrifice; the sacrificial hearth (on which the sacred fire is kept); — a heap, quantity; — season, opportunity.
v.t. To spread, &c. = bithrānā, biṭālnā, q.q.v. v.t. To spread, scatter, strew; to sprinkle; to bespatter; to splash, spill; to waste.
more: v. a. To scatter, to sprinkle.

def: v.t. To cause to pass, to pass, spend (time).
more: v. a. S. व्यतीत. To pass, spend (time) ( guzārnā ). Sakhī sab ghar ba-ghar baṅgle chhawāweṅ, Piyā ke saṅg meṅ nis din bitāweṅ . Song Bārā-māsā . Burnished and bright are all the houses round, In love's delights the maidens with their mates abound.
(trans of بتنا or بيتنا) v. a. To pass.
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. Young branch of a tree, new shoot or sprout; branch; tree, bush; clump, cluster, tuft; — spreading, expansion.

s.m. Spreading, extension, &c. = vi-stār, q.v. s.m. Spreading; spread, extension, expansion, diffusion, development (i.q. bistār); — breadth, amplitude; vastness, expanse; — copiousness, abundance; — particularizing at length, detail, amplification, prolixity; — diameter (of a circle); — the branch of a tree with its new shoots; a shrub
subs: adv. In detail, at length, fully, &c.

def: s.m. Giving up, abandoning; donation, gift, charity, alms.
more: s. m. Giving charity, donation, alms.

def: v.t. To give away, bestow, grant.
s.m. Little son, &c. = biṭwā, q.v.
s.m. = biṭaurā, q.v.
more: n. m. A heap of cakes of dried cow-dung plastered on the outside to protect them from rain. 1. Biṭauṛe meṅ se uple hī nikleṅge . Prov. From the dung-heap only cakes of dung can come. 2. Sūjhe na biṭauṛā, chāṅd se "Rām Rām ." Prov. He cannot see the dung-heap before his nose, and the new moon he salutes "Rām Rām." 3. Girgaṭ kī dauṛ biṭauṛā . Prov. The lizard's refuge is the dung-heap. Slang. 4. But, biṭauṛā banā baiṭhā hai . He sits as senseless as a statue or a dung-heap.

def: s.m. Heap of cakes of dried cowdung (used for fuel).
more: s. m. A heap of the cakes of dried cow-dung.

def: s.f. A tax on shopkeepers, &c. in a village.
s.f. Excrement, dung (especially of birds).
s.f. Disquietude, agitation; pain, agony; anguish, ache; distress, misery, trouble.
s.m. Annular cushion put on the head to carry a pot of water, &c. (syn. bīṅḍā; inḍwā).
more: s. m. An annular cushion put on the head to carry a pot of water on.

def: n. f. S. व्यथा 1. A sad story; a tale of misery; a tragedy. Bāle-pan kī suno bithā, jis din dhartī līyā janam, Sunat pitā jī mukh morin, aur sust hue sab log kuṭam . Song. Lāonī . The story of my childhood, hear, the day that I had birth, My father turned his face aside, gloom came upon our hearth. (Disappointment at the birth being a girl). 2. Hardship; affliction; misfortune ( āfat ).
s.m. Ant-hill.
v.n. To be alarmed, scared, &c. = bijhaknā, q.v. v.n. To be startled, scared, frightened; to start; to take fright (cf. jhijhaknā, bidakna, bhićaknā).
v.t. = biṭhānā, q.v. (cf. also the forms biṭhlānā, baiṭhānā, baiṭhālnā).

v. a. S. उपविष्ट from उपवशि् to sit down. 1. To locate; station; pitch. 2. To set; place; seat; lay; stow; deposit. Anḍoṅ pe biṭhānā . 3. To make a child sit on a pot de chambre , or go to stool. Wom. Bachche ko biṭhā le, nahīṅ kapṛe bharegā . Wom. 4. To make one sit down; give one a seat. Barābar biṭhānā . 5. To lodge; post; house; install. Yeh do dil ko ek jā biṭhātā nahīṅ, Kisī kā ise vasl bhātā nahīṅ . Mīr Hasan. Two hearts the envious sky ne'er brings together, It likes not happy unions with each other. 6. To appoint a watch; place on guard. Apne māl pe ādmī biṭhāo . 7. To set, fix, stick in; insert (a stone) ( jaṛnā ). Nagīnā biṭhānā . 8. To thrust in; drive in or home ( utārnā ). Pech biṭhānā . 9. To plant; sow ( lagānā ). Paudā biṭhānā . 10. To put in its proper place; bring in. Maiṅ ne Amjad Alī Shāh kī jagah Wājid Alī Shāh ko biṭhā dīyā . * Gālib. 11. To enter one (in a school, or shop). 12. To establish; fix; appoint; institute; constitute ( muqarrar k .). Jāgīr meṅ kisī ne zar-rez mulk pāyā, Kar band-o-bast apnā nazm o nasaq biṭhāyā . Nazīr. 13. To assess; levy; impose ( lagānā ). Ṭikas biṭhānā . 14. To settle one in business; bind (an apprentice). Sau rupaë de-ke use biṭhā dīyā . 15. To enthrone; install; raise to the throne. Takht pe biṭhānā . 16. To inculcate; impress; imbue; instill; inspire. Aisā bhaë hogā parjā ko, aisā dāb baṭhāwegā, Chor, jār, gunḍā, baṭmārā ḍhūṅḍā nāhīṅ pāwegā . W. Rus. Song on the Prince of Wales. Such fear and awe he 'll strike that though they now abound, Thieves, rakes, roués, and ṭhags , tho' sought will ne'er be found. 17. To make one believe ( yaqīn dilānā ); to influence. Dil meṅ biṭhānā. Jāne uske dil meṅ kyā biṭhā dīyā? 18. To keep as a mistress or kept-woman. Ranḍī ko ghar meṅ biṭhā līyā . 19. To keep at home (a marriageable or married daughter); to refuse to give in marriage. Apn/?/ bachchī ko biṭhā rakhtī, na tum ko detī, Jān Sāhab, maiṅ agar jāntī aīyāsh tumheṅ . Wom. Jān Sāhab. 20. To gather; collect; realize ( ugāhnā ). 21. To cause to be assembled; convene ( ikhaṭṭā k .); convoke. Panchāyat biṭhānā . 22. To cause to congeal; solidify; harden. Pārā biṭhānā . 23. To cause to be formed into lumps. Chāṅwal biṭhā dīye . 24. To cause to sink or fall in. Ek ghūṅse meṅ āṅkh biṭhā dī . 25. To stuff ( ṭhūsnā ); press (cotton etc.). 26. To reduce; bring down; contract; condense. Das ser dūdh meṅ do ser kundā biṭhāyā . Halwāīs. 27. To level; plane; make flat, even. Ṭhok-pīṭ-ke biṭhā dīyā . 28. To bring down; destroy; demolish ( ḍhānā ). Meṅh ne ghar biṭhā dīyā . 29. To melt; dissolve; make sloppy or thin. Barsāt ne sārā guṛ biṭhā dīyā . 30. To cause to sink or founder ( ḍubānā ). Tūfān ne jahāz biṭhā dīyā . 31. To ruin; destroy; bring to nought; do for (a person). Ek hī sadme ne biṭhā dīyā . 32. To cause the heart to sink; to break one's heart. Dil biṭhānā . 33. To stun; to cause to faint. Ek ghūṅse meṅ biṭhā dīyā . 34. To set in order; arrange; adjust; fix. 35. To proportion; suit; adapt; dove-tail. Joṛ biṭhānā . 36. To calculate; compute; strike a balance ( phailānā ). 1. Hisāb biṭhānā . 2. Mīzān biṭhānā . 37. To bring out a result; make out; produce. Ser kā savā ser biṭhāyā . 38. To hazard; chance; stake; bet with or upon ( aṛnā ). (Gamblers). Dāoṅ pe biṭhā dīyā . 39. To pledge; pawn; mortgage ( girvī dharnā ). (Gamblers). 40. To gamble away ( hārnā ). (Gamblers). Sārā māl juë meṅ biṭhā dīyā .

v.t. To cause to enter; to enter, place (in, -meṅ, as a boy in a school); to pierce, penetrate, thrust in, drive home, press down, ram down, pack closely, stuff, fix firmly; — to cause to be seated, cause to sit, to seat; to settle; to set (as a stone in a ring, &c.), to plant, sow; to cause to congeal, coagulate, solidify; to cause to cake, form into lumps; to locate, station, lodge; to instal; to establish, fix, set up; to cause to settle down; to lay (as dust, &c.), to allay, calm, assuage; to set (over, -par, as a watch, guard, &c.); to fix or impress (on, -meṅ, the mind), to implant (in the mind), impress (the mind with an idea), strike (terror, &c. into the mind); to set in order, settle, arrange, adjust; to make out (a calculation, &c.), strike (a balance); to establish, get up, cause to be convened (a panćāyat, &c.); to come into possession of, gain, realize, gather, collect; to make (the heart) sink or faint (dil biṭhānā)
more: (caus. of بيٿهنا) v. a. To cause to sit down, to seat, to set, to settle.
subs: v.t. (intensive of biṭhānā, but often used in the sense of biṭhānā), To cause to fall in, cause to sink or founder; to demolish, destroy, raze, level, ruin; to melt, dissolve; to settle, knock down, stun, &c. (with, -meṅ, a blow); to stake, bet (upon, -par); to gamble away, lose
v.t. To keep seated; to detain; to keep (a marriageable or married daughter) at home; to refuse to give in marriage; — biṭhā-lenā, v.t. To make (one) sit = biṭhānā (generally in an intensive sense).

def: s.f. Brushing or cleansing the warp ( = bithrī).

ety: bithār˚ = Prk. वित्थारे(इ) = S. विस्तारय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+स्तृ
def: v.t. To spread, scatter, &c. ( = bithrānā, q.v.; and see also bitharnā, of which it is the caus.).
more: (caus. of بتهرنا) v. a. To cause to scatter, to cause to sprinkle.

def: v.n. To be spread, sown, scattered; to be sprinkled, splashed, &c.
more: Poet. v. n. To be scattered ( bikharnā ); to spread. Bāl hain bithre, āṅkh unīdī, Shām savere utṭhe rī . Song. Brij. Shām early rose with hair dishevelled and a liquid langour in his eyes.
(perhaps from विस्तरण r. स्तृ Spread over, cover) v. n. To be scattered, to be sprinkled, to be spread.

def: v.t. To cause to be spread, &c.
s.f. Row, line; road; terrace in front of a house; stall, shop; — a sort of drama; a division of the planetary spheres (comprising three asterisms).

v.t. = biṭhānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to enter; to enter, place (in, -meṅ, as a boy in a school); to pierce, penetrate, thrust in, drive home, press down, ram down, pack closely, stuff, fix firmly; — to cause to be seated, cause to sit, to seat; to settle; to set (as a stone in a ring, &c.), to plant, sow; to cause to congeal, coagulate, solidify; to cause to cake, form into lumps; to locate, station, lodge; to instal; to establish, fix, set up; to cause to settle down; to lay (as dust, &c.), to allay, calm, assuage; to set (over, -par, as a watch, guard, &c.); to fix or impress (on, -meṅ, the mind), to implant (in the mind), impress (the mind with an idea), strike (terror, &c. into the mind); to set in order, settle, arrange, adjust; to make out (a calculation, &c.), strike (a balance); to establish, get up, cause to be convened (a panćāyat, &c.); to come into possession of, gain, realize, gather, collect; to make (the heart) sink or faint (dil biṭhānā)
more: (caus. of بيٿهنا) v. a. To cause to sit down, to seat, to set, to settle.
subs: v.t. (intensive of biṭhānā, but often used in the sense of biṭhānā), To cause to fall in, cause to sink or founder; to demolish, destroy, raze, level, ruin; to melt, dissolve; to settle, knock down, stun, &c. (with, -meṅ, a blow); to stake, bet (upon, -par); to gamble away, lose
v.t. To keep seated; to detain; to keep (a marriageable or married daughter) at home; to refuse to give in marriage; — biṭhā-lenā, v.t. To make (one) sit = biṭhānā (generally in an intensive sense).

def: v.n. (local) = baiṭhnā, q.v. v.n. To seat oneself, sit down, be seated, be unemployed or idle; to sit, brood, incubate; to alight, settle, perch; to ride (on, -par); to subside, settle, abate; to pair, couple (a bird); to obtain employment or service; to be laid out or expended (on, -par); to visit a person in grief for the purpose of condolence; to sit in dharnā (q.v.)
more: v. n. (dakh.) To sit: see بيٿهنا baiṭhnā.
subs: v.n. To sit down; to settle down; to fall in or down (a house, wall, &c.).

def: v.t. To spread, scatter, strew; to sprinkle; to bespatter; to splash, spill; to waste.
more: Poet. v. a. S. विस्तरण from स्तृ to spread over. To waste; scatter ( bakhernā ); throw about; spill ( khinḍānā ). Kuchh khāyā, kuchh bithrāyā . Song.
v. a. To scatter, to sprinkle.

def: s.f. = bithārī, q.v. s.f. Brushing or cleansing the warp ( = bithrī).
E. n. f. Cleansing the warp.
part. adj. Passed, elapsed, gone, spent, bygone
more: adj. S. व्यतीत Passed; gone; elapsed; bygone. Barsan bhaë batīt, pīyā ke darshan ko rī . Song.

def: v.n. To pass, elapse, &c. ( = bitīt honā, bītnā); to happen (to), to betide.
more: v. n. To pass; elapse; go by.

def: s.f. Little daughter; daughter.
more: (dimin. of بيٿي) s. f. A daughter.

def: s.m. Black salt, a particular kind of fetid salt (obtained by fusing fossile salt with emblic myrobalans, and used medicinally).
s.m. A coarse species of pulse (of the māsh, or of the kalāya kind).
more: s. A coarse species of pulse of the kalāya kind: see S̱. کلاٴي kalāʼī.

def: v.n. To pass; to come to pass, &c. = bītnā, q.v.
more: v. a. S. बट् to divide. 1. To realize. Kaho āj kyā baṭṭā? 2. To share; gain; obtain (as a profit). Rupaë ke saude meṅ chār paise baṭṭe .

v. n. 1. To be divided, shared, distributed. 2. To be dispersed, scattered, separated. Khawāseṅ jo thīṅ rū-barū, haṭ gaīṅ. Bahāne se har kām ke baṭ gaīṅ . Mīr Hasan. 3. To be distracted, drawn off (attention). Dhyān baṭnā .
v. a. S. वट् to surround. To twist; to form by convolution (thread).
n. m. An instrument with which ropes are twisted.
v. n. To come to pass.

ety: bīt˚ = Prk. वित्ते(इ), or वित्त(इ), fr. वित्त = S. वीत, p.p.p. rt. वी
def: v.n. To pass, elapse, expire (as time); to come to pass, take place, occur, happen, befall, arrive; to undergo, suffer, endure
more: v. n. S. ब्यातीत 1. To pass over or away; elapse; go by; run out; expire; to be past and gone. 1. Bītī tāhi bisār de, āge kī sudh leu . Girdhar Kuṅdlī. Forget the past, regard the future. 2. Kalyāṅ man meṅ sochat haiṅ, aur phūl khile kumlāvat haiṅ, Jo din un par bīt gaë, ab so din ham par āwat haiṅ . Dohā. The young buds in their minds revolve the full-blown flowers' decay, The evil fate which these assails will come on us one day. 2. To take place; befal; happen; occur; come to pass; come about. 3. To feel; suffer; bear; endure. Jis pe bitī ho ye wohī jāne . Jān Sāhab.
v. n. To pass, come to pass, happen, befall: see بتنا bitnā.
subs: v.n. To pass away, pass over, &c.

ety: bīṭ˚ = Prk. विट्टे(इ), or विट्ट(इ), fr. विट्ट (for विट्ठ) = S. व्यस्त, p.p.p. of rt. वि+अस
def: v.t. To pour out, spill; to scatter.
more: v. n. To spill, to scatter.

def: s.f. = bhiṭnī, q.v. s.f. Nipple (of a woman's breast).
v.t. To scatter, &c. = biṭānā, q.v. (cf. the forms ḍubānā and ḍubonā).
more: 1. v. a. To scatter, to sprinkle. 2. s. m. A son: see بٿوا biṭawā.

def: s.f. Detailed settlement = bila-bandī, q.v. s.f. An account of the settlement of a district (specifying the name of each maḥal, the farmer of it, and the amount at which it is let, &c.).
v.t. To bespatter, &c. ( = bithrānā, q.v.); to asperse, calumniate, vilify, denounce; to belie.
more: v. a. 1. To find fault; pick holes ( aib nikālnā ). Bhale chaṅge kām ko bitrātā hai . 2. To impute falsehood ( jhuṭlānā ); deny; belie. Mujh ko bitrātā hai .

def: s.m. Wealth, riches, property, possessions, money, substance, thing; power, ability, means, resources
more: + Wom. n. f. S. वित्तम power. 1. Means ( bisāt ); ability; power ( bal ); reach (pauṅhch); resources. Dān bit samān . Prov. Let your charity be as your means. 2. Money; riches; wealth; substance. 3. Bulk; magnitude; size; height ( uṅchāī ). Ek bittā kā bit nū, nau bittā kī ḍāḍhī . Prov. Not a span high, and a beard nine spans long! bit bāhar , adj. Out of one's power.
subs: adj. Out of (one's) power, beyond (one's) means.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بت (bat, “mark, score”).

def: s.m. Span, hand's breadth.
more: n. m. S. वितस्ति A span; a hand's breadth. bit'tyā , n. m. A span high ( bālishtiyā ); a dwarf.

def: adv. Explicitly, in detail, &c. ( = biʼt-taṣrīḥ); minutely, item by item, particularly.
more: adv. Particularly; at full length; item by item.

def: adv. Especially, particularly, specifically.
adv. Little by little, bit by bit, piecemeal, in detail; by degrees; gradually.
adv. In detail; expressly, distinctly, explicitly.
more: adv. In detail; expressly; explicitly; distinctly.

def: s.f. A boys' game (a boy throws forward a small round potsherd with his foot, which another boy has to bring back with his breath held, and crying bittī, bittī); a run.
more: n. f. H. bittā span. A boys' game, so called from bittī a small round potsherd held under the great toe and thrown forward. This potsherd has to be brought back by one of the competitors with his breath held all the time, crying bittī bittī . Hence, a run ( dauṛ ). Jo hār gaë, to sau bittiyāṅ leṅge . If you fail the penalty is a hundred runs.
n. f. A pair; couple; match ( joṛ ). biṭ'ṭī budnā , v. n. To couple; match; form pairs.

def: s.f. Pair, couple, match
subs: v.n. To couple, match, form pairs.

def: s.m. The melon ( = ḵẖarbuza); the water-melon, Cucurbita citrullus.
s.m. lit. 'A span high'; a dwarf (syn. bālishtiyā).
more: s. m. A dwarf (i. e. a span high).

ety: S. वटु+कः; or dim. of beṭā
def: s.m. Little son; dear son; little boy, child.
v.t. To cause to be measured; to have (one) measured or fitted.
more: Mah. v. a. Caus. of بيونتنا q. v.
v. a. Caus. of بتنا q. v.

def: s.m. Marriage ( = byāh, q.v. s.m. Marriage
subs: s.f. The marriage fees of a Brāhmaṇ
byāh kar-denā (-se), To marry (one, to), to marry or take a wife (e.g. ćār byāh kiyā, he took four wives); to celebrate a marriage
byāh lenā, byāh le-ānā, byāh le-jānā (-ko), To marry, take in marriage, bring home a wife

def: s. f. A kibe, blister or chap on the foot, chilblain.
more: s. f. Sowing, the act of sowing, seedtime.

def: s.f. Kibe or chap on the heel; chilblain
more: n. f. S. विपादिका A kibe or chap on the heel, generally caused by cold; a chilblain. Jiske na phaṭī biwāī, woh kyā jāne pīṛ parāī? Prov. Who never in his heel a chap did feel, To him another's pain did ne'er appeal. bivāī phaṭnā , v. n. To suffer from chilblains.
subs: v.n. To suffer from kibes, or from chilblains.

def: s.m. = bimān, q.v. s.m. Car or chariot of the gods (sometimes serving as a seat or throne, and at others carrying them through the skies self-directed and self-moving); car or vehicle (generally); ornamental bier (on which a Hindū corpse is borne to the place of cremation).
s.f. Judicial decree according to Hindū law ( = bewasthā; fatwā).

more: s. f. The first sale for ready money in the morning.

def: s.f. = bohnī, q.v. s.f. Handsel; first sale for ready money early in the day (considered as a good omen and as determining the luck of the day. — The etymology is doubtful: some trace the word to bhawānī, because ascribed to the auspices of that goddess; others derive it from the root vr̤idh).

n. f. H. behvār transaction, or S. वृध् to increase. Hansel; the first money received during the day, or the first ready-money sale by shopkeepers or hucksters, no credit being given as a rule for the article first sold. ☞ The practice says Elliot, "is universal in India, and is precisely like the handsel of England," which Lemon in his Dictionary, explains to be, "the first money received at market, which many superstitious people will spit on, either to render it tenacious that it may remain with them and not vanish away like a fairy gift, or else to render it propitious and lucky that it may draw more money to it." 1. Pahlī bohnī, Allāh miyāṅ kī ās . Prov. The first money is to hand, for the rest my hope is in God. 2. Bohnī ṭhonī, rad balā . Prov. The first sale made, ill luck hath fled.
s.m. A man who (having no plough of his own) works with a borrowed plough, spade, &c.

Just listen noisy drum, the daughter-in-law, your rival's come. 2. Sonā bhī dīnā, rūpā bhī dīnā, bābal, motī bhī dīnā, Dīnā jaṛat jaṛāo, ek na dīnī sir kī kaṅghī, bābal, Morī sās nanad bole bol . Wom. Song. Maṅḍhā . My father, you have given me gold and pearls and silver too, And jewels richly set, one comb is all forgot by you, My husband's mother's, sister's taunts each day I hear anew. 3. A phrase; part of a sentence; sentence. 4. A verse; a measured line. 5. Good name; character; honor; credit. Panchan meṅ merī pat rahe, sakhyan meṅ rahe bol, Sāīṅ se sāṅchī rahūṅ, bāj bāj re ḍhol! Dohā. Liv…

Taunt ( bolī , 5). 2. Ridicule; raillery. bolī, yā bolī ṭholī mārnā, pheṅknā, yā kasnā, avāze tavāze pheṅknā , v. a. 1. To use inuendoes; to say sharp or bitter things ( chuṭkiyāṅ lenā ); to speak at one; taunt; sneer. Mātā, bagaṛ beṛe kī nār mārtīṅ bolī, Jī apne meṅ bhunūṅ, lage tan golī . Wom. Play. O mother mine, the women ne…
s. f. 1. Speech, dialect, language. 2. Conversation, talk, saying. بولي کرنا bolī karnā, To jeer, to jest.

ety: bol˚ = Prk. वोल्ल(इ), or लोल(इ); S. वद(ति), rt. वद्; or, prob. S. ब्रूय(ते), pass. of rt. ब्रू, corrupted to बूर्य(ते)
def: v.n. To speak, talk, tell, say, utter, pronounce, declare, give utterance to; to bid (at an auction); to cry (one's wares); to give forth sound, to sound, to bark (as a dog, &c.), chirp (as a bird, and the like, according to the animal); to strike (as a clock, &c.); to crack (with a sound), split, break; to be brisk or active (as a market)
more: v. n. 1. To sound; give or emit sound. 2. E. To strike (the hour). Chār bole ānā . 3. To crack; break; smash. 4. To chirp; chirrup; twitter. Chiṛīmār ṭolā, bhāṅt bhāṅt kā jānvar bolā . Prov. It is a bird-mart where voices diverse you may hear. (A concourse various, opinions various). 5. To speak; talk; open one's lips; give tongue or utterance to; break silence. Phr. To have a tongue in one's head; to fall from, or escape one's lips; to speak one's mind. Bolī bolīṅ, to ye bolīṅ, "merī jūtī bole." Prov. If she e'er spoke at all, it was, 'my shoes may speak.' 6. To tell; say; declare; assert. 7. To cry one's wares ( āwāz lagānā ). 8. To bid; make a bid (at an auction). 9. ( Rus .) To call; hail; summon; invite. Wā he bol le nā? Rus. Why dont you call out to him? 10. To prescribe; lay down; direct. Pahre pe kiskī naukrī bolī gaī? 11. To answer; reply; respond. 1. Wāṅ jā-ke kyā tum bologe? S…

(perhaps, from s. r. वद Speak) v. n. 1. To speak, talk. 2. To sound, emit sound, to articulate. 3. To tell, to say. بول اٿهنا bol uṭhnā, To speak out, to exclaim. بولناچالنا bolnā-chālnā, To converse, speak together. بولي سوبولنا bole so bolnā, To speak repeatedly.
subs: v.n. To leave word; to order (as goods, &c.)
v.n. To speak out, exclaim, cry out
v.n. To leave word (with, or at); to be proclaimed (on change), to be bankrupt; to be over, come to an end; to become; old or worn out, give signs of decay, &c.; to crack, split, break (as a vessel)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀩𑁄𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀇 (bŏllaï). Also compare بُلَانَا (bulānā, “to call”).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Saying; speaking, talking (e.g. boltī mainā, 'a talking mainā); — s.m. Faculty of speech; life, soul; a thing which sounds; pipe or ḥuqqa (in the language of faqīrs).
more: n. m. 1. The organ or faculty of speech. 2. Life; soul; the inner man ( boltā pursh ). 1. Ye jab boltā apnā so jāëgā, Yahīṅ apnā pyālah bhī ho-jāëgā . Inshā. When this my life shall rest in sleep, My funeral feast here will they keep. 2. Boltā hai jab talak hai boltā . Prov. While there is life there is speech. 3. Bolte kī āshnāī hai . Prov. Attachment ends with life. 4. Bolte ko jān le, pahchān le, Boltā jo kuchh kahe so mān le . Mendicant's cry. Your soul right know, discern it, pray, Whate'er the soul doth say, its voice obey. 3. A huqqā or pipe. (Faqīrs' idiom.) Safar meṅ boltā sāth ho, to jahāṅ chāhā chilam bhar lī . Your huqqā in your journeys take, at will, your chilam filled, your smoke you take.
adj. Speaking; talking. Boltā chākar munīb ke āge gūṅgā . Prov. Your servant fluent though with all to speak. Before his master will strict silence keep.
part. act. (from بولنا used substantively) The faculty of speech; soul, life.

def: s.m. Stack of straw; heap of chaff; a hollow bamboo; a drill; — the whole shell of a gourd, or of a cocoa-nut.
s.f. = bohnī, q.v.
subs: s.f. Idem. s.f. Handsel; first sale for ready money early in the day (considered as a good omen and as determining the luck of the day. — The etymology is doubtful: some trace the word to bhawānī, because ascribed to the auspices of that goddess; others derive it from the root vr̤idh).

def: s.f. Sowing; season of sowing, seed-time.
more: s. f. The season of sowing.

def: s.m. Stalk, stem; stump ( = būṅṭ); stopple; — finger, &c. = boṭ.

1. adj. Deep. 2. s. m. Studs of gold or silver.

def: s.m. Small bells for the pā-zeb or ornament worn on the feet (so called from their shape resembling the fruit of the jujube); studs of gold or silver; — vanity, conceit, pride
subs: (par), To be vain or conceited (of), to boast, make a display, show off.

def: s.m. Dip; fish-bait.

more: adj. 1. Broken. Boṛā kūāṅ . 2. Toothless ( khoṅḍā ).

def: s.m. A mat made of palm leaves.

def: v.t. = boṛnā, q.v.; & = bolnā, q.v.
more: v. a. Rus. To dip ( ḍobnā ); drench; steep ( bhigonā ). 1. Gulāl lipaṭ gayo mukh par, sārī raṅg meṅ borī, Sāṅvre, mo se khelo na horī . Holī Song. 2. Phaiṅṭ gulāl, hāth pichkārī, bor daī sab ghūṅgaṭ meṅ rī . Holī Song.

W. v. n. Pr. vuṭṭa . To be dipped, immersed, drowned. Beṛā bāṅdho sarap kā bhau-sāgar ke māṅh, Chhāḍe to woh būḍiyāṅ, gahe to ḍasyāṅ bāṅh . Kabīr. A raft of snakes you bind, in mid stream drift; let go, And you are drowned; hold fast, your arm is bitten so.

more: v. a. To cause to dive, to immerse, drown, sink.

more: n. m. lit . a bull. ( Ast .) Taurus.
s. m. A bull; Taurus (the sign of the zodiack). ¶ barash or barsh (for वर्ष ) s. m. Rain; a year.

def: s.m. Bull, ox, bullock ( = br̤ish).
s. m. A bull.
s.m. = br̤ihaspati, q.v.

n. m. varan a syllable. A term in prosody. It consists of four metrical lines, or charans counted by syllables. It is of three kinds— 1. Sam even, having an equal number of syllables in each line. 2. ardh-sam half even, having an equal number of syllables in the first and third, and second and fourth lines. 3. bisham odd, having a different number of syllables in each line.

s.f. State, condition; means of subsistence, livelihood, allowance, stipend, pension, income, estate, property, endowment; a religious endowment or grant; behaviour, conduct; right, custom, privilege; — supporters, clients
subs: s.m. A kind of Hindī metre (consisting of four metrical lines or ćaraṇs, counted by syllables. It is of three kinds: 1˚ sam, or 'even,' having an equal number of syllables in each line; 2˚ ardh-sam, 'half even,' having an equal number of syllables in the first and third, and in the second and fourth lines; 3˚ bisham, 'odd,' having a different number of syllables in each line).

def: s.m. = br̤itā, q.v.
s.m. A religious endowment; a grant (generally of land) to a religious person, or to a tenant, on certain stipulations.
adj. & adv. Abortive, vain, useless worthless; in vain, uselessly, idly, needlessly, without proper cause, gratuitously; foolishly; — s.m. A useless or worthless thing; a nullity, dead letter.
more: adj. S. वृथा, H. be without, arth value. Worthless; useless; purposeless ( akārath ); vain. 1. Yeh sansār birthā hai . 2. Birthā dhan jo dīn na dīnā, Biddyā lehī updesh na kīnā . Behāri Lal. Vain is the wealth from which the poor no gain derive, The learning vain which did instruction never give. bir'thā , adv. 1. Uselessly; in vain; unnecessarily; fruitlessly. 2. Incorrectly; wrongly; falsely. bir'thā , n. m. A dead letter; a nullity.

def: s.m. Appointing; a proxy in the performance of religious duty.
more: s. m. Appointing; a proxy in the performance of religious duties.

def: s.m. Occurrence, incident, event; tidings, information, rumour, report, intelligence; tale, story, narrative, relation, history, account; topic, subject; mode, circumstance, manner, state, condition; sort, kind; difference
more: n. m. 1. Story; history; account; narration; description. 2. Statement; assertion; explanation.
s. m. Intelligence, tidings, narration, story, history, topick, mode, circumstance.
subs: s.m. The record of a decision given by a panćāyat.

def: s.f. = būʼā, and bū-bū, qq.v. s.f. Sister, dear sister; father's sister, aunt (syn. phūphū; phūʼā; cf. bū-bū).
more: E. n. f. Cont. of bhūt a goblin. Bo; an exclamation used to frighten children.
n. f. P. بوبُيدن to smell. Smell. See باس bū phūṭnā , v. n. 1. To be spread (a smell). 2. To be spread (news); to be betrayed or divulged ( khul jānā ); transpire; get wind.
(for ابو Heb. ) Father; possessed of, endowed with. بوعلي سينا bū aﻌlī sīnā or بو علي bū aﻌlī, Avicenna.
(in India, bo ) s. f. Smell, scent, odour. بو کش bū-kash, Smelling, smeller. بو کشي bū-kash-ī, s. f. Taking a smell, inhaling odour.
adj. mas. and بوه bavva, adj. fem. Foolish, silly.

def: s.f. = būʼī, q.v. s.f. Goblin, bogie (a word used to frighten children).
s.m. Father; possessor, &c. (used in comp.; — before words defined by the Arabic article al, it is shortened in pronunciation to bu; e.g.); — buʼl-ʻajab, adj. Wonderful, marvellous
subs: adj. Given to excess, or to exaggeration; loquacious; prodigal
adj. & s.m. Desirous; whimsical, capricious, fickle; — a wavering, or fickle person; blockhead

s.m. lit. 'Father of wailing'; the heron; the bittern
adj. & s.m. Of various hues, variegated, varied, various; changeable; wonderful; — anything of various hues; a chameleon.

ety: Pehl. boi; Zend baoidhī, rt. bud = S. budh
def: s.f. Odour, scent, smell; trace, soupçon, small portion, particle (in India, bo commonly signifies a bad smell, bū being used for 'smell' generally)
subs: (-kī), Smell (of a thing) to be diffused; soupçon, trace, or inkling (of an affair, &c.) to get abroad; to be divulged, to leak out, get wind
bo-dār, adj. & s.m. Scented, fragrant; — a kind of scented leather
adj. & s.m. Smelling, sniffing; — one who smells or inhales the scent or odour (of a thing)
s.f. Taking a smell; inhaling odour.

def: s.f. Sister, dear sister; father's sister, aunt (syn. phūphū; phūʼā; cf. bū-bū).
more: n. f. Mah. Dear sister. Father's sister; aunt ( phuppī ). Hin.
s. f. 1. Sister. 2. ( in Hindawī ) An aunt by the fatherʼs side.

def: s.m. An old fool, old dotard; simpleton, blockhead ( = buṛbhas, q v.).
more: E. n. m. būṛhā old. An old fool; a simpleton; a stupid. Chupke denī khol kunḍī, le na Inshā ko bulā, Ḍar bhalā kyā chāhiye darbān būbak kā tujhe? Inshā.

def: adj. Ancient, perpetual, everlasting.
s.f. Desire for food, hunger.
adj. Desirous of food, hungry.
n. m. The husk of bājrā , etc.
s.f. Sister, elder sister; lady ( = bī-bī); favourite concubine, or one of superior rank.
more: n. f. 1. An elder sister. 2. A lady. Yeh bach-pane se hai apnī hālat ki jiskī hotī hai chāh, būbū, Phir us se kartī hai apne maqdūr āh! bandī nibāh, būbū . Wom. Raṅgīṅ.
n. f. The breasts; bubbies ( chūṅchī ).
s. f. 1. A sister. 2. (on the west of India) A lady (for بي بي bībī ). 3. A favourite concubine or one of superiour rank.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Ear-cropped; without ears; — a man, or a dog, without ears.
more: adj. Ear-cropped; without ears. bū'chā , n. m. būchī , n. f. A man or dog without ears. Ātā nibṛā, būchā saṭkā . Prov. The flour spent, the dog off went.
s. m. A kind of sedan, a chair pālkī.

def: adj. f. Without ears, ear-cropt.
v.n. To contract and put back (the ears, as a horse).

more: v. n. To sit in a confined posture, to crouch.
